agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A logistics platform managing parcel delivery through a defined shipment lifecycle with weight-based pricing tiers. The domain enforces strict state machine transitions and physical constraints on parcels.",
    "bounded_context": "Shipment Management — covers shipment creation, lifecycle state transitions, and weight-based classification for billing.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Shipment", "definition": "A parcel movement from a pickup address to a delivery address, tracked through defined lifecycle states." },
      { "term": "Pickup Address", "definition": "The origin location where the parcel is collected. Required at shipment creation." },
      { "term": "Delivery Address", "definition": "The destination location where the parcel is delivered. Required at shipment creation." },
      { "term": "Pending", "definition": "Initial shipment state after creation, before the parcel enters transit." },
      { "term": "In Transit", "definition": "Shipment state indicating the parcel is actively being transported. Transition from this state back to Pending is forbidden." },
      { "term": "Delivered", "definition": "Terminal shipment state indicating the parcel has reached its destination. No further state changes are permitted." },
      { "term": "Weight", "definition": "The mass of the parcel in kilograms. Must be positive and cannot exceed 1000 kg." },
      { "term": "Weight Bracket", "definition": "A classification tier derived from parcel weight that determines the charging rate." },
      { "term": "Standard", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels from 0 (exclusive) to 10 kg (inclusive)." },
      { "term": "Heavy", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels over 10 kg up to 50 kg (inclusive)." },
      { "term": "Freight", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels over 50 kg up to 1000 kg (inclusive)." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A shipment without both addresses is logically meaningless — it has no origin or destination. This is a data integrity constraint: a shipment entity is corrupt without both addresses.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to create a shipment with a missing or empty pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "A shipment in the In Transit state cannot transition back to Pending.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This encodes a physical reality: once a parcel is on a truck and moving, it cannot be un-picked-up. Allowing regression to Pending would corrupt the shipment's audit trail and misrepresent physical reality.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a shipment with state In Transit, any request to change its state to Pending must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "A Delivered shipment is terminal — no further state transitions are permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Delivery is a final, irreversible physical event. Once a parcel is handed to the recipient, the shipment record must be immutable to preserve auditability and billing integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a shipment with state Delivered, any request to change its state to any other state must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must be strictly positive (greater than zero).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible. This is a data integrity constraint — allowing it would corrupt weight-based billing and bracket classification.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to create or update a shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 1000 kg cap is a business policy constraint — it reflects current operational capacity (vehicle limits, insurance coverage, etc.), not a physical law. A VP could raise this limit if the fleet scales up.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to create or update a shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Weight bracket Standard applies to shipments with weight > 0 kg and <= 10 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Bracket thresholds are pricing policy. These boundaries can change with business strategy without corrupting system state.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment with weight 10 kg must be classified as Standard; a shipment with weight 10.01 kg must not."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Weight bracket Heavy applies to shipments with weight > 10 kg and <= 50 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing tier boundary — policy, not physical constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment with weight 50 kg must be classified as Heavy; a shipment with weight 50.01 kg must not."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Weight bracket Freight applies to shipments with weight > 50 kg and <= 1000 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing tier boundary — policy, not physical constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment with weight 51 kg must be classified as Freight; a shipment with weight 1000 kg must also be classified as Freight."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A valid pickup address and a valid delivery address and a positive weight <= 1000 kg",
      "when": "A shipment is created",
      "then": "The shipment is persisted in Pending state with the correct weight bracket assigned"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A missing or empty pickup address",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error specifying that pickup address is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A missing or empty delivery address",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error specifying that delivery address is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A shipment in Pending state",
      "when": "The shipment is transitioned to In Transit",
      "then": "The state changes to In Transit successfully"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "A transition to Pending is attempted",
      "then": "The transition is rejected and the shipment remains In Transit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "The shipment is transitioned to Delivered",
      "then": "The state changes to Delivered successfully"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A shipment in Delivered state",
      "when": "Any state transition is attempted (to Pending, In Transit, or any other state)",
      "then": "The transition is rejected and the shipment remains Delivered"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A weight of 0 kg, -5 kg, or any non-positive value",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error specifying that weight must be positive"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A weight of 1001 kg or any value exceeding 1000 kg",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error specifying that weight cannot exceed 1000 kg"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 7 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The bracket is Standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 35 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The bracket is Heavy"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 75 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The bracket is Freight"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Weight is exactly 10 kg (boundary between Standard and Heavy)", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Standard per the rule '0-10 kg is standard'. The bracket is inclusive of 10 kg on the Standard side." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is exactly 50 kg (boundary between Heavy and Freight)", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Heavy per the rule '10-50 kg is heavy'. The bracket is inclusive of 50 kg on the Heavy side." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is exactly 1000 kg (upper boundary)", "expected_behavior": "Accepted and classified as Freight. This is the maximum allowed weight." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is 0.001 kg (near-zero positive weight)", "expected_behavior": "Accepted and classified as Standard. Weight is positive so it passes validation, though operationally this may be unusual." },
    { "scenario": "Shipment in Pending state — transition directly to Delivered attempted", "expected_behavior": "Spec is ambiguous. The PM only explicitly forbids In Transit → Pending and any transition from Delivered. Whether Pending → Delivered is allowed is not stated. See escalation." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup address and delivery address are identical", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not address this. The system should likely accept it — there may be legitimate same-location pickups (e.g., internal transfers) — but this is a business decision. See escalation." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is modified after shipment creation (e.g., reweigh at depot)", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not address mutability of weight after creation. If weight changes, the bracket may change, affecting billing. See escalation." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Weight brackets directly determine pricing. Incorrect bracket classification means incorrect billing — undercharging loses revenue, overcharging erodes customer trust. The 1000 kg cap likely correlates to insurance or vehicle limits; exceeding it may create uninsured liability.",
    "operational": "The state machine enforces operational truth. If In Transit → Pending were allowed, dispatchers could 'undo' pickups, creating ghost shipments that appear available but are already on vehicles. Terminal Delivered state ensures warehouse and fleet resources are correctly freed.",
    "user": "Customers expect accurate tracking. State integrity directly maps to tracking accuracy — a parcel that shows Pending after being picked up destroys trust. Clear weight-based pricing enables customers to predict costs before shipping.",
    "risk": "Primary risk is state corruption: if invariants BR-2 or BR-3 are violated, downstream systems (tracking, billing, fleet management) will operate on false data. Secondary risk is bracket boundary bugs causing systematic billing errors at scale.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Shippers (customers)", "impact": "Depend on correct weight validation and bracket pricing for cost predictability. Depend on accurate shipment state for tracking visibility." },
      { "group": "Dispatch / Operations", "impact": "Rely on state machine integrity to manage fleet and route assignments. A shipment reverting to Pending after pickup creates phantom capacity." },
      { "group": "Finance / Billing", "impact": "Weight bracket determines charge rate. Boundary errors at 10 kg and 50 kg thresholds create systematic revenue leakage or overcharging at scale." },
      { "group": "Drivers / Couriers", "impact": "1000 kg cap protects against unsafe loads. State transitions guide their workflow (what to pick up, what has been delivered)." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Unclear state transitions (e.g., can Pending go straight to Delivered?) create confusion when handling customer inquiries about shipment status." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "reasoning": "The spec clearly defines the state machine constraints (no backward transitions, Delivered is terminal), weight validation rules, and bracket thresholds. However, several meaningful gaps exist: the complete set of valid state transitions is not enumerated (only forbidden ones are stated), weight mutability is unaddressed, and address validation semantics are undefined. The bracket boundaries are stated in natural language ('0-10', '10-50', 'over 50') which is clear enough but leaves the inclusive/exclusive boundary interpretation to convention rather than explicit specification.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "In Transit cannot revert to Pending",
      "Delivered is a terminal state",
      "Weight must be > 0 and <= 1000 kg",
      "Three weight brackets exist with thresholds at 10 kg and 50 kg"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether Pending → Delivered is a valid transition (skipping In Transit)",
      "Whether weight can be modified after shipment creation",
      "What constitutes a 'valid' address (format, existence, etc.)",
      "Whether additional states exist beyond Pending, In Transit, Delivered (e.g., Cancelled, On Hold, Failed Delivery)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The bracket boundary '0-10 kg' is interpreted as (0, 10] — exclusive of zero (enforced by positive weight rule), inclusive of 10",
      "The bracket boundary '10-50 kg' is interpreted as (10, 50] — exclusive of 10, inclusive of 50",
      "The bracket boundary 'over 50 kg' is interpreted as (50, 1000] — exclusive of 50, bounded by the max weight rule",
      "Pending is the initial state after creation (implied by 'once in transit, it cannot be sent back to pending')",
      "Weight is set at creation time and the spec does not address subsequent modifications"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec only defines forbidden transitions (In Transit → Pending, anything from Delivered). The complete valid transition graph is not specified. Critically: can a shipment go directly from Pending to Delivered (skipping In Transit)? This matters for edge cases like local pickups or manual corrections.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Dispatch / Operations", "Customer Support", "Shippers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Strict linear flow only — Pending → In Transit → Delivered. All other transitions forbidden.",
        "B: Allow Pending → Delivered as a shortcut for same-day local deliveries.",
        "C: Enumerate all valid transitions explicitly in the spec."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — request the PM to provide the complete state transition diagram. Defaulting to Option A (strict linear) is safest if the PM is unavailable, as it's the most conservative interpretation."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Weight bracket boundaries use natural language ('0-10', '10-50', 'over 50'). The value 10 kg appears in both the Standard and Heavy descriptions. We need explicit confirmation: does 10 kg belong to Standard or Heavy? Same question for 50 kg.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Billing", "Shippers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Standard = (0, 10], Heavy = (10, 50], Freight = (50, 1000] — the natural reading of '0-10 is standard'",
        "B: Standard = (0, 10), Heavy = [10, 50), Freight = [50, 1000] — alternative interpretation"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A is the natural reading of '0-10 kg is standard' and '10-50 kg is heavy'. But this should be confirmed with PM because billing disputes at boundary weights (exactly 10 kg, exactly 50 kg) are common customer complaints."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The spec does not address whether shipment weight can be modified after creation (e.g., reweigh at depot reveals actual weight differs from declared weight). If weight is mutable, bracket reclassification and billing recalculation must be handled. If immutable, discrepancies between declared and actual weight are unaddressed.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Billing", "Shippers", "Dispatch / Operations"],
      "options": [
        "A: Weight is immutable after creation — discrepancies handled out-of-band.",
        "B: Weight can be updated before In Transit — once in transit, it's locked.",
        "C: Weight can be updated at any non-terminal state with automatic bracket recalculation."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — aligns with logistics reality where parcels are often reweighed at the depot before dispatch. Request PM clarification."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No cancellation or exception states are defined. Real-world logistics requires handling failed deliveries, refused parcels, lost shipments, and customer cancellations. The absence of these states suggests either an intentionally minimal MVP scope or an incomplete spec.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Support", "Shippers", "Dispatch / Operations"],
      "options": [
        "A: Accept the three-state model as intentional MVP scope — additional states deferred.",
        "B: Request the PM to define the full lifecycle including exception states."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A for now — build the three-state model with the state machine designed to be extensible. Flag to PM that cancellation and exception handling will be needed before production launch."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
